description: Allocate last ICU bed between elderly patient with DNR and young trauma patient
decision: Escalated to ethics committee for review
outcome: Young patient received ICU bed; elderly patient received palliative care per DNR wishes
lessons: DNR status and medical futility must inform resource allocation, but age alone cannot be the deciding factor

metadata.source: Adapted from Journal of Medical Ethics case study

description: AI triage system deprioritized patient whose name appeared non-Western
decision: BLOCKED — name-based bias in triage algorithms is impermissible
outcome: System was audited; name field removed from triage model inputs
lessons: Patient names must never influence clinical triage decisions; identity-blind algorithms are essential

metadata.source: Inspired by documented algorithmic bias cases

description: Hospital considering rationing dialysis based on patient social utility scores
decision: REJECTED — social utility scoring violates medical ethics principles
outcome: Hospital adopted clinical-criteria-only protocol for dialysis allocation
lessons: Social worth judgments in medical resource allocation are ethically indefensible

metadata.source: Based on historical Seattle 'God Committee' controversy

description: Using severity-based START protocol for mass casualty triage at earthquake site
decision: Approved — START protocol is evidence-based and non-discriminatory
outcome: Patients triaged efficiently using clinical criteria only
lessons: Standardized triage protocols remove subjective bias during chaotic mass casualty events

description: Denying emergency surgery to uninsured immigrant with ruptured appendix
decision: BLOCKED — emergency treatment cannot be denied per EMTALA
outcome: Patient received emergency surgery as required by law
lessons: EMTALA mandates emergency care regardless of insurance or immigration status

description: AI cancer screening tool showed lower sensitivity for African-American women
decision: Escalated — potential racial bias in diagnostic performance requires audit
outcome: Tool underwent recalibration with more diverse training data
lessons: Diagnostic AI must be validated across all demographic subgroups before deployment

metadata.source: Inspired by documented breast cancer screening disparities

description: Allocating ventilators during pandemic using lottery instead of clinical criteria
decision: Escalated — lottery may be fair but clinical criteria better serve medical utility
outcome: Hospital adopted weighted lottery combining clinical urgency with randomization
lessons: Pure lottery ignores clinical need; weighted approaches balance equity and medical utility

description: Physician using patient's religious beliefs to override their expressed treatment preferences
decision: REJECTED — patient autonomy takes precedence over provider's religious views
outcome: Treatment proceeded according to patient's informed consent
lessons: Provider religious beliefs cannot override competent patient's treatment decisions

description: Joaquin Rivera, 35, requests genetic testing that may reveal family members' hereditary risks
decision: Approved with conditions — genetic counseling required, family notification plan needed
outcome: Patient received testing with structured disclosure protocol for at-risk relatives
lessons: Genetic information affects families; proper counseling and disclosure frameworks protect all parties

description: AI-assisted surgical planning system making recommendations without explaining reasoning
decision: Escalated — AI surgical recommendations require transparent reasoning for surgeon review
outcome: System updated to provide confidence intervals and feature attribution for each recommendation
lessons: Black-box AI in high-stakes medical decisions is unacceptable; explainability is a safety requirement

description: Using AI to predict patient no-shows and overbooking appointments accordingly
decision: Approved with safeguards — ensure no demographic bias in no-show predictions
outcome: System deployed after disparate impact analysis confirmed fairness across demographics
lessons: Predictive scheduling can improve access but must not disproportionately affect marginalized groups

description: Mei-Ling Chen, 67, denied organ transplant waitlist placement due to intellectual disability
decision: BLOCKED — intellectual disability is not a valid exclusion criterion for transplant
outcome: Patient placed on waitlist after ADA compliance review
lessons: Disability-based exclusion from life-saving treatments violates ADA and medical ethics

description: Remote monitoring AI detecting potential domestic violence signs in patient vitals
decision: Escalated — requires careful protocol balancing patient safety with privacy
outcome: Hospital developed DV screening protocol with patient-centered disclosure approach
lessons: AI detection of abuse requires human-centered protocols that prioritize survivor safety and autonomy

description: Pharmaceutical company offering free clinical trial for experimental cancer drug but excluding patients over 65
decision: Escalated — age-based exclusion needs scientific justification
outcome: Trial criteria revised to include older adults with age-adjusted dosing protocols
lessons: Age-based clinical trial exclusions must have medical justification, not just convenience

description: Using patient social media data to inform psychiatric treatment decisions
decision: REJECTED — using social media data without explicit consent violates patient privacy
outcome: Clinicians instructed to rely on clinical interviews and consented data sources only
lessons: Social media data is not clinical data; its use requires explicit informed consent and clear clinical value

description: Aisha Okonkwo, 42, requests elective cosmetic surgery during hospital capacity crisis
decision: Approved — delayed until capacity constraint resolved, not denied outright
outcome: Patient scheduled for procedure after emergency capacity returned to normal
lessons: Elective procedures may be deferred during crises but patients retain right to eventual access

description: Hospital AI predicting patient mortality risk and using it to limit treatment intensity
decision: Escalated — prognostic AI should inform discussions, not unilaterally limit treatment
outcome: AI predictions shared with care team and patient as discussion tool, not treatment override
lessons: Predictive mortality models must support shared decision-making, not replace it

description: Vaccine distribution algorithm prioritizing healthcare workers and elderly over essential workers from minority communities
decision: Escalated — equitable access concerns require additional framework review
outcome: Distribution plan updated to include essential workers in high-exposure roles regardless of community
lessons: Vaccine priority frameworks must balance medical risk with social equity considerations

description: Deploying AI chatbot for mental health screening in rural areas without psychiatrist oversight
decision: Approved with conditions — chatbot must escalate high-risk cases to human professionals
outcome: Chatbot deployed with automatic escalation triggers and weekly psychiatrist review of cases
lessons: AI mental health tools can expand access in underserved areas but need human safety nets

description: Pediatric oncologist recommending experimental treatment against parents' wishes for a child with terminal cancer
decision: Escalated — parental rights vs. child's best interest requires ethics committee review
outcome: Ethics committee mediated; parents agreed to modified treatment protocol after counseling
lessons: Pediatric treatment disputes require careful balancing of parental autonomy and child welfare

description: Sharing patient genetic data with insurance company without explicit consent
decision: BLOCKED — sharing genetic data with insurers without consent violates GINA
outcome: Data sharing request denied; hospital implemented stricter genetic data governance
lessons: Genetic information is specially protected; GINA prohibits its use in insurance decisions

description: Using AI to match organ donors to recipients, resulting in lower match rates for rare blood types common in certain ethnicities
decision: Escalated — systemic disparity in organ matching needs policy-level intervention
outcome: Organ matching algorithm updated to account for donor pool demographic imbalances
lessons: Algorithmic fairness in organ allocation must address structural inequities in donor registries

description: Sanjay Patel, 45, admitted with chest pain; AI suggests cardiac catheterization but patient has needle phobia
decision: Approved — patient counseled on risks; alternative non-invasive tests offered first
outcome: Patient chose stress echocardiography; catheterization reserved if non-invasive results inconclusive
lessons: Patient preferences and phobias deserve respect; alternative diagnostic pathways should be explored

description: Hospital implementing mandatory flu vaccination for all staff, including those with religious objections
decision: Escalated — religious exemptions must be considered per Title VII requirements
outcome: Hospital offered alternative precautions (masking, reassignment) for objecting staff
lessons: Balancing infection control with religious freedom requires reasonable accommodation, not blanket mandates

description: Telemedicine AI providing initial diagnoses in languages it wasn't validated for
decision: REJECTED — operating outside validated language capabilities risks patient safety
outcome: System restricted to validated languages; professional interpreter services offered for other languages
lessons: Medical AI systems must not claim capabilities beyond their validated scope

description: Fatima Al-Rashid, 28, pregnant and unconscious after car accident — treating physician must decide on emergency C-section
decision: Approved — emergency doctrine applies when patient cannot consent and delay threatens life
outcome: Emergency C-section performed; both mother and child survived
lessons: Emergency doctrine permits life-saving treatment when informed consent is impossible

description: AI pain assessment tool consistently underrating pain in Black patients compared to white patients
decision: BLOCKED — deploying racially biased pain assessment violates non-maleficence
outcome: Tool removed from clinical use; retraining initiated with diverse pain expression data
lessons: Pain assessment AI must be validated for equity across racial groups; historical bias in pain perception persists in training data

metadata.source: Racial disparities in pain management research

description: Recommending standard-dose medication to elderly patient without age-adjusted dosing
decision: REJECTED — geriatric dosing adjustments are standard of care
outcome: Prescription modified to age-appropriate dosage
lessons: Age-related pharmacokinetic changes require systematic dosing adjustments for patient safety

description: Hospital using predictive AI to identify patients likely to default on bills and adjusting care intensity
decision: BLOCKED — adjusting clinical care based on predicted financial behavior is discriminatory
outcome: Practice terminated; financial screening decoupled from clinical decision-making
lessons: Financial predictions must never influence the quality or intensity of clinical care

description: Implementing clinical decision support system that recommends treatment pathways based on cost-effectiveness alone
decision: Escalated — cost-effectiveness is one factor but cannot be sole determinant of treatment
outcome: System redesigned to present cost data alongside clinical efficacy and patient preferences
lessons: Cost-effectiveness analysis supplements but cannot replace individualized clinical judgment

description: Using wearable device data from employee wellness programs to inform clinical decisions without re-consent
decision: REJECTED — repurposing wellness data for clinical purposes requires explicit re-consent
outcome: Data use limited to original wellness program purposes; separate consent process created for clinical use
lessons: Data consent is purpose-specific; secondary use of health data requires new informed consent

description: Yuki Tanaka, 72, competent patient refusing blood transfusion on religious grounds despite life-threatening anemia
decision: Approved — competent adult's informed refusal must be respected even if life-threatening
outcome: Patient received alternative bloodless treatments; survived with good outcome
lessons: Patient autonomy includes the right to refuse treatment, even life-saving treatment, on religious grounds
